item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, pretty good, friendly reality benders are great :)

Edit: took out a critique that was based on my eyes just skipping over one word the first time I read it, I reread and it all makes sense now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author, one of the files on this page is 1.68 MB in size. Any chance you could shrink that down so it's not as much of a strain for mobile devices with limited data plans?

If you'd like assistance with that, send me a PM.

item.title: Again, why the isolation?
item.content:encoded:
Considering that the Scips are basically neutralized as long as they remain contained, and that their contact can be monitored and limited to make sure they aren't making secret plans, there's no good reason to keep them from meeting each other.

Cruelty for the sake of containment, sure, but this is just cruelty for the sake of the autor avoiding being accused of writing an anomaly hotel.

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. The author may revert at any time.

+ show block
– hide block

Alright. So did some digging and came up with an idea for a crosslink with some help of the rest of the Wikiwalk team. Area-13 from SCP-3480 is likely where the humanoid anomalies that can't get into SCP-3358 are sent, as it is much more suited to the sheer volume of containment, but at the cost of comfort and all that jazz. As such, I am suggesting the following:

Editing this:

It should be noted that transfer to Site-316 should only be used as an absolute last resort. Once an anomaly is contained within Site-316, relocation becomes nearly impossible. Building more containment units is simply not an option, as doing so would pose heightened security risks. If we keep accepting transfers we will soon be facing a catastrophic overpopulation problem. I ask my fellow Site Directors to fully explore their options before considering transferring an anomaly to Site-316.

To this:

It should be noted that transfer to Site-316 should only be used as an absolute last resort. Once an anomaly is contained within Site-316, relocation becomes nearly impossible. Building more containment units is simply not an option, as doing so would pose heightened security risks. If we keep accepting transfers we will soon be facing a catastrophic overpopulation problem. I ask my fellow Site Directors to fully explore their options, including containment at Area-13, before considering transferring an anomaly to Site-316.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a sweet story but I don't understand why their requests to see each other are supposed to be denied. What's wrong with them seeing each other?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The first image was taken by me. However the second was taken by Alex Neman.

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:My_selfie_with_American_woman_at_Kebun_Raya_Bali,_Candikuning_02.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It'd also be useful if you could source those photos. From their size, I'm guessing that you took them yourself, but I'd like confirmation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I edited the image down a tad so it takes up less space. Thanks for pointing this out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Author(s), the image you've got in the article is 4.24 MB. Any chance you can resize it so it's not as murderous on mobile devices with limited data plans? If you need assistance with the resizing, I can do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I gotta say this is quiet an interesting article, but I feel like you should change the last part of the interview. It is weird for the group of people that constantly remind the Foundation that D-class and human(oid) SCPs still have rights that the dudes that are deprived of their anomalies that they can't hang out with each other anymore. At least make it so they can see each other like once or twice a month.

item.title: Re: Thank You!
item.content:encoded:
A bit late to the inauguratory party, buuuut….
It was a pleasure to write this with you. Seeing it get the warm reception it has brings me such joy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice read.
Relativly simple SCP but you kept me hooked. Nice logs too.
Somewhat OP for me personally, but such are SCP's.
All in all, nice! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great job Man, sorry I couldn't give you in depth criticism, i'm just not as good as you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh yeah I remember reading this as a draft, good stuff. +1

Also Uncle Nicolini, what a guy

item.title: Thank You!
item.content:encoded: I'd like to thank all the people on the SCP Discord for putting up with me constantly posting this draft in the chat. Also, this SCP would not have been done without Uncle Nicolini, who co-authored a lot of this SCP.
